content:
How to Apply to University of Mpumalanga (UMP)

Thinking about studying at University of Mpumalanga (UMP)? Based in Mbombela (Nelspruit), Mpumalanga, UMP is a Comprehensive University established in 2014. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: UMP Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameUniversity of Mpumalanga (UMP)
TypeComprehensive University
LocationMbombela (Nelspruit); Siyabuswa campus
Year Founded2014
Application Portalhttps://www.ump.ac.za/apply/
Application FeeR150
Applications OpenMay 1
Applications CloseOctober 31
Minimum APS24 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone013 002 0011
Contact Emailinfo@ump.ac.za

Why Choose UMP?

SA's newest comprehensive university, bridging the Mpumalanga education gap.

  • Only the second new university established in post-apartheid South Africa (alongside SPU)
  • Located near the Kruger National Park, making its nature conservation and tourism programmes uniquely placed
  • Siyabuswa campus serves the former KwaNdebele homeland communities in the Highveld
  • Agriculture programmes focus on subtropical and banana belt farming unique to Mpumalanga

Faculties and Programmes at UMP

UMP offers qualifications across 4 faculties:

  • Agriculture & Environmental Sciences
  • Education
  • Hospitality & Tourism Management
  • Natural Sciences

Popular programmes include: BSc Agriculture, BEd, BA Tourism Management, BSc Nature Conservation, Diploma: Hospitality Management.

APS Requirements for UMP

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at UMP is 24 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: 24–32 depending on programme

UMP requires a minimum APS of 24 for most programmes. As a young university, it has a small student body with excellent lecturer-to-student ratios. Hospitality Management has strong industry placement rates.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to UMP

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.ump.ac.za. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the UMP online application portal at https://www.ump.ac.za/apply/. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of R150.

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in May 1 and close on October 31. Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from UMP, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for UMP

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: May 1
  • Application Deadline: October 31
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at UMP

Studying at UMP can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • NSFAS
  • UMP Financial Aid
  • Mpumalanga Provincial Government Bursaries
  • SANParks Bursary Programme

NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme): If your combined household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

Preparing for UMP While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at UMP to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to UMP, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing UMP With Other Universities

Not sure if UMP is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorUMPTypical Alternative
TypeComprehensive UniversityTraditional University / UoT
LocationMbombela (Nelspruit)Varies
Minimum APS2420–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeR150R100–R400
NSFAS EligibleYesYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to UMP

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to UMP.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet UMP's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.
keywords: University of Mpumalanga (UMP) how to apply
quickAnswer: To apply to University of Mpumalanga (UMP), visit https://www.ump.ac.za/apply/, pay the R150 application fee, and submit your documents before October 31. Minimum APS is 24 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to UMP?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at UMP is 24 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (24–32 depending on programme). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the UMP website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for UMP?
faqs.answer: Applications for UMP typically open in May 1 and close on October 31. We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.ump.ac.za/apply/ to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for UMP?
faqs.answer: The application fee at UMP is R150. Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the UMP website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does UMP accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, UMP is an accredited NSFAS institution. If your household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding which can cover tuition, accommodation, meals, transport, and a living allowance. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to UMP?
faqs.answer: For your application to UMP, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
